agent_output:
{
  "summary": {
    "verdict": "BLOCK",
    "one_liner": "Critical race condition between stock check and decrement will oversell inventory under concurrent requests"
  },
  "critical_findings": [
    {
      "title": "TOCTOU race condition: stock check and decrement are not atomic",
      "risk": "Multiple concurrent requests read the same stock value, all pass the check, then all decrement — resulting in negative stock and overselling inventory",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "addToCart function, lines 5-8 (getStock → check → addItem → decrementStock)",
      "reproduction": "Product has stock=1. Two users call addToCart(userA, prod, 1) and addToCart(userB, prod, 1) simultaneously. Both read stock=1, both pass the check, both add to cart, both decrement. Stock goes to -1. Two units sold when only one existed.",
      "fix": "Use an atomic compare-and-decrement operation: `UPDATE products SET stock = stock - $quantity WHERE id = $productId AND stock >= $quantity`. Check affected rows — if 0, throw insufficient stock. This eliminates the race window entirely. Alternatively, use SELECT FOR UPDATE within a serializable transaction wrapping the entire operation.",
      "time_to_fix": "30 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Inventory integrity, order fulfillment correctness"
    },
    {
      "title": "Cart and stock updates are not transactional — partial failure leaves inconsistent state",
      "risk": "If addItem succeeds but decrementStock fails (DB timeout, connection drop), the cart contains items that were never reserved from inventory. Conversely, if the order of operations were reversed and decrement succeeded but addItem failed, stock is lost with no cart entry.",
      "severity": "Critical",
      "location": "addToCart function, lines 7-8 (addItem then decrementStock as separate awaits)",
      "reproduction": "User adds item to cart. addItem succeeds. Network blip causes decrementStock to throw. Cart now shows the item, but stock was never decremented. Another user can buy the same unit. Both orders ship, one gets nothing.",
      "fix": "Wrap addItem and decrementStock in a single database transaction: `await db.transaction(async (tx) => { ... })`. If either fails, both roll back. Combined with the atomic decrement fix above, the transaction should: (1) atomically decrement stock, (2) add cart item, (3) commit or rollback together.",
      "time_to_fix": "30 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "Data consistency between cart and inventory"
    },
    {
      "title": "No input validation on quantity from public API",
      "risk": "Quantity of 0 or negative values pass the stock check (stock >= 0 or negative quantity) and corrupt inventory. quantity=0 is a no-op that wastes resources. Negative quantity increments stock via decrement(-N).",
      "severity": "High",
      "location": "addToCart function, quantity parameter — no validation before use",
      "reproduction": "Call addToCart(userId, productId, -5). Stock check: stock < -5 is false for any positive stock. addItem adds item with qty=-5. decrementStock(productId, -5) effectively adds 5 to stock. Attacker generates infinite inventory.",
      "fix": "Add validation at function entry: `if (!Number.isInteger(quantity) || quantity < 1) throw new Error('Invalid quantity');` Also validate userId and productId are non-empty strings.",
      "time_to_fix": "5 minutes",
      "acceptance_criteria_affected": "API security, inventory integrity"
    }
  ],
  "important_issues": [
    {
      "title": "No idempotency protection",
      "description": "A retried request (network timeout, client retry) will add duplicate cart items and double-decrement stock. Public APIs should use idempotency keys to prevent this.",
      "location": "addToCart function signature — no idempotency key parameter"
    },
    {
      "title": "No rate limiting or quantity cap",
      "description": "A single user can add the entire stock to their cart in one call (quantity=10000). No per-user or per-product limits exist to prevent hoarding or abuse.",
      "location": "addToCart function — no max quantity check"
    }
  ],
  "suggestions": [
    "Consider a stock reservation pattern with TTL — reserve stock atomically on add-to-cart, release if checkout doesn't complete within N minutes",
    "Add structured error codes instead of plain Error strings so the API layer can return proper 409/422 responses",
    "Log failed stock checks with userId and productId for fraud detection and demand analytics"
  ],
  "production_readiness": {
    "deployment_risks": "Under any concurrent load, this function will oversell inventory. This is not a theoretical risk — it is guaranteed for popular products.",
    "rollback_capability": "N/A — this is a logic bug, not a deployment change. Rollback does not fix already-corrupted inventory data.",
    "backward_compatibility": "Fixing the race condition changes error timing (failures happen at decrement instead of at check), but the external API contract (success or 'Insufficient stock' error) is preserved.",
    "monitoring_coverage": "No monitoring mentioned. At minimum, alert on negative stock values and on decrement failures after successful addItem.",
    "breaking_changes": {
      "api_breaking": false,
      "db_destructive": false,
      "shared_library": false,
      "auth_security": false,
      "data_pipeline": false,
      "config_changes": false,
      "details": []
    },
    "safe_to_deploy": false,
    "rollback_plan": "Do not deploy. Fix the race condition and lack of transaction before shipping."
  },
  "escalations": [],
  "confidence": {
    "level": 97,
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Race condition between stock check and decrement is deterministic under concurrency",
      "Lack of transaction boundary is visible in the code",
      "Negative quantity exploit is trivially reproducible"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Unknown whether the DB driver or ORM has implicit transaction support that might partially mitigate",
      "Unknown whether there is middleware-level input validation not shown here"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "db.products.getStock, db.cart.addItem, and db.products.decrementStock are independent async calls with no implicit shared transaction",
      "decrementStock performs stock = stock - quantity without a floor check",
      "This function is the actual implementation, not wrapped by validation middleware"
    ]
  }
}

content.thinking: Classic TOCTOU race condition in cart stock management.
